$schema: Core Design Model — technology-independent intermediate representation (IR)
description: Archetype-aware IR. Technology Adapter maps IR → one resolved design skill pack.

backend-service.notes: Full DDD with aggregates per bounded context. Map every capability and dataEntity to a context.

web-ui.notes: userExperience.screens and navigation are primary. boundedContexts optional unless epic implies domain complexity.

mobile-ui.notes: Include device/offline concerns in userExperience and integrations when epic implies them.

integration-service.notes: integrationFlows[] with steps, sources, targets, transforms, error handling — not backend DDD unless epic requires it.

data-pipeline.notes: dataPipelines[] with sources, mappings, transforms, DQ rules, scheduling.

rules: No technology names in IR (no Controller, Screen, Flow, @Service, DataWeave, React)
Read technology_context.archetype.id and apply matching archetypeRules
Use generic layer assignments: presentation, business, data, integration, configuration
Business rules use BR-ID and business language only — no pseudo-code in IR
Legacy migration stores source excerpt in legacyLogicMigration[].sourceLanguage — not target stack language
API operations are resource-oriented: method, path, operationId, request, response, errors
userExperience uses generic terms: screen, view, journey, navigation — not component library names
